title: agentify-audit-review
description: Schema for the structured-review file produced by /agt-self-improve. Designed as a strict superset of the JSON contract REVIEW_PROMPT.md emits (verdict, headline counts, severity-bucketed findings, references) so REVISE_AGENTIFY_PROMPT.md can consume audit output without changes — provided the synthetic-review marker (per WS-F-003) is recognized and gates apply behind a mandatory human-review step. Mandatory: every finding has at least one URL citation fetched in the audit run AND a falsifiable acceptance criterion.

schema_version.description: Audit-review schema version. Bumped on breaking change.

audit_id.description: Unique identifier for this audit run (typically an ISO timestamp like '2026-04-28T15:00:00Z' or a UUID).

produced_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the audit completed.

skill.description: Producer skill name. Must be 'agt-self-improve' (configurable per the WS-F-002 SKILL.md if other auditors emerge).

version.description: agentify version at the time of the audit (e.g., 'v4.2'). Used to date-stamp findings against the relevant feature set.

model.description: Optional Claude model identifier that ran the audit (e.g., 'claude-opus-4-7').

synthetic_source.description: Marker identifying this review as machine-produced rather than human-authored. Mirrors the HTML comment '<!-- agentify-synthetic-review-source: self-improve -->' that REVISE_AGENTIFY_PROMPT.md (WS-F-003) recognizes. Constraining to 'self-improve' means /agt-feedback-derived findings (WS-G) get a different synthetic_source value if they ever travel through this schema.

verdict.description: Aggregate verdict. Mirrors the LOOP_PROMPT.md REVIEW JSON contract.

critical.description: Count of critical findings (would block ship).

major.description: Count of major findings (significant; should fix soon).

moderate.description: Count of moderate findings (worth attention this iteration).

strategic.description: Count of strategic findings (cross-cutting; may span releases).

findings.description: Per-finding records. Each finding MUST cite at least one URL (web-fetched in the audit run) and provide a falsifiable acceptance criterion (otherwise REVISE cannot reliably apply or verify).

id.description: Stable finding ID (e.g., 'AUDIT-001'). Severity prefix optional ('CRIT-001', 'MAJ-001'). The /agt-self-improve skill assigns these per audit run.

severity.description: Mirrors the LOOP_PROMPT.md REVIEW severity buckets.

description.description: Full prose. Should explain (a) what's currently wrong/stale/missing in agentify, (b) what the cited source(s) say has changed, (c) why this matters.

references.description: URLs the audit fetched and that support the finding. At least one required. Each MUST have been web-fetched in this audit run (not invented from training data) — the audit must be able to attest to having read each URL.

snippet.description: Optional: relevant excerpt from the fetched content (≤ 500 chars).

acceptance_criterion.description: Falsifiable criterion the next REVISE iteration must satisfy to close this finding. Should be a runnable command, a grep pattern, a file existence check, or similar — not 'feels right'. REVIEW (next iteration) verifies via this criterion. Examples: 'grep -q new-feature-name AGENTIFY.md', 'test -f hooks/new-hook.sh', 'jq -e .new_field agentify-config.schema.json'.

caused_by_prior_revise.description: True if this finding is a regression introduced by a recent REVISE pass (vs. a pre-existing or external-source-driven issue). Used by LOOP_PROMPT.md §C7 REGRESSION exit condition.

feedback_issue_id.description: Optional: when this finding originates from /agt-feedback ingestion (WS-G), the source GitHub issue ID. null for pure online-research findings.

audit_inputs.description: Optional metadata about what the audit consumed. Useful for triage and reproducibility.

context_bundle_files.description: Paths to context/*.md files re-checked for staleness.

stale_threshold_days.description: Threshold (in days) above which 'Last verified' entries are flagged for re-check.

feedback_issues_consulted.description: Number of open agentify-feedback GitHub issues consulted via lib/feedback_ingest.sh (WS-G-004).

external_sources_fetched.description: Number of distinct external URLs fetched (Claude Code docs, GitHub issues, Anthropic blog, etc.).
